name: extraction-intelligence
goal: Fix systemic duplication and low-signal issues in the meeting extraction pipeline with two-layer LLM dedup, real confidence filtering, and broken plumbing fixes

userStories.title: Prompt hardening — self-review guidance and exclusion sections
userStories.description: Add decision/learning exclusion guidance and self-review instructions to the normal-mode extraction prompt. File: packages/core/src/services/meeting-extraction.ts, function: buildMeetingExtractionPrompt() (line 552). Add sections after line 624 (action item EXCLUDE list). Read the existing prompt structure first — mirror the action item INCLUDE/EXCLUDE pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: buildMeetingExtractionPrompt() output contains '## What is NOT a decision' section with 4+ EXCLUDE examples
buildMeetingExtractionPrompt() output contains '## What is NOT a learning' section with 4+ EXCLUDE examples
Rules section contains self-review instruction: 'Before finalizing, review your list'
Unit test: prompt string contains all three new sections
Existing prompt tests pass unchanged

userStories.title: Add confidence schema to decision/learning extraction
userStories.description: Change the prompt JSON schema to request { text, confidence } objects for decisions and learnings. Add confidence guides. File: packages/core/src/services/meeting-extraction.ts. Update schema at lines 603-606 and add guides after line 631. Also update light-mode prompt (buildLightExtractionPrompt, line 657) for learnings only.
acceptanceCriteria: Normal-mode prompt schema shows decisions as [{ 'text': '...', 'confidence': 'number (0-1)' }]
Normal-mode prompt schema shows learnings as [{ 'text': '...', 'confidence': 'number (0-1)' }]
Prompt contains '## Decision Confidence Guide' with 4 tiers
Prompt contains '## Learning Confidence Guide' with 4 tiers
Light-mode prompt learnings schema also uses { text, confidence } format
Unit test: prompt contains confidence guides

userStories.title: Parse confidence from LLM response for decisions/learnings
userStories.description: Update parseMeetingExtractionResponse() to handle both string and { text, confidence } objects. Add decisionConfidences and learningConfidences parallel arrays to MeetingIntelligence type. File: packages/core/src/services/meeting-extraction.ts, lines 838-861 (parse loops) and line 61 (MeetingIntelligence type). The RawExtractionResult type at line 109 already supports Array<string | { text?, confidence? }>.
acceptanceCriteria: MeetingIntelligence type has decisionConfidences?: number[] and learningConfidences?: number[]
Parser handles string-only responses: decisions extracted, confidences default to undefined
Parser handles object-only responses: text and confidence both parsed
Parser handles mixed string/object arrays
Parser handles objects with missing confidence: text extracted, confidence undefined
Parser handles confidence out of range: clamped to [0,1] or rejected
Parser handles empty arrays gracefully
rawItems entries include parsed confidence for debugging
Existing parse tests pass unchanged (backwards compat)
warnings: Pre-mortem R2: LLM may return old string format, new object format, or a mix. Parser must handle all variants. Test all 6 parse variant cases.

userStories.title: Use real confidence in processing for decisions/learnings
userStories.description: Replace hardcoded 0.9 confidence in processMeetingExtraction() with real values from parallel arrays. File: packages/core/src/services/meeting-processing.ts, lines 402-443. Change for-of loops to indexed loops to access parallel confidence arrays.
acceptanceCriteria: Decisions loop uses intelligence.decisionConfidences?.[i] ?? 0.9
Learnings loop uses intelligence.learningConfidences?.[i] ?? 0.9
Decision with confidence 0.4 filtered out (below 0.65 threshold)
Decision with confidence 0.7 gets status 'pending' (above 0.65, below 0.8)
Decision with confidence 0.9 gets status 'approved' (above 0.8)
Same three threshold tests for learnings
Backwards compat: undefined decisionConfidences defaults to 0.9
Existing processing tests pass unchanged
warnings: Pre-mortem R1: MeetingIntelligence type change from task-3 must be complete first.

userStories.title: Extend garbage/trivial filters to decisions and learnings
userStories.description: Apply isGarbageItem() to decisions and learnings during parsing. Add isTrivialDecision() and isTrivialLearning() functions. File: packages/core/src/services/meeting-extraction.ts. Add filter functions after line 217. Apply in parse loops at lines 838-861.
acceptanceCriteria: isGarbageItem() called for each decision and learning in parse loop
Garbage items produce validationWarnings entries
isTrivialDecision() matches: 'We discussed the roadmap', 'Meeting moved to Tuesday'
isTrivialDecision() does NOT match items with decision verbs: 'We discussed and decided to ship Friday'
isTrivialLearning() matches personal trivia patterns
Normal decisions/learnings pass through unfiltered
Both positive (should filter) and negative (should keep) test cases
warnings: Pre-mortem R3: Trivial filters could false-positive on legitimate items. Keep patterns conservative (anchored to start, check for decision verbs before rejecting).

userStories.title: Load committed items from memory files
userStories.description: Add parseMemoryItems() to parse .arete/memory/items/decisions.md and learnings.md. Wire into loadReconciliationContext() to replace hardcoded empty recentCommittedItems. File: packages/core/src/services/meeting-reconciliation.ts, lines 594-615. Read packages/core/src/integrations/staged-items.ts appendToMemoryFile() first to understand the write format.
acceptanceCriteria: parseMemoryItems(content, defaultSource) function exported
Handles section format: ## Title + date + source + text
Handles ISO 8601 dates with time component
Returns Array<{ text, date, source }>
Filters to last 30 days, caps at 100 items
Returns empty array for null content (missing file)
Returns empty array for empty content
Skips malformed sections gracefully
loadReconciliationContext() loads from memory files via StorageAdapter
recentCommittedItems populated (no longer hardcoded [])
Unit tests for all edge cases
warnings: Pre-mortem R5: Read appendToMemoryFile() in staged-items.ts first to verify exact write format. Use StorageAdapter.read(), never fs.

userStories.title: Batch LLM quality review function and integration
userStories.description: Add batchLLMReview() for semantic dedup and quality filtering. Integrate into backend (agent.ts after reconciliation) and CLI (--reconcile path). Files: packages/core/src/services/meeting-reconciliation.ts (new function), packages/apps/backend/src/services/agent.ts (integration after ~line 335), packages/cli/src/commands/meeting.ts (CLI integration), packages/core/src/services/index.ts (barrel export). Read parseMeetingExtractionResponse() lines 729-746 for JSON extraction pattern to reuse.
acceptanceCriteria: batchLLMReview(currentItems, committedItems, callLLM) exported from meeting-reconciliation.ts
Prompt includes committed items, current items with IDs, DROP/KEEP criteria
Returns Array<{ id, action: 'drop', reason }>
Handles valid JSON: correct items flagged
Handles JSON with markdown fences: strips and parses
Handles malformed JSON: returns empty drops, logs warning
Handles invalid IDs: silently skips
Handles empty drops: all items kept
Backend: called after reconciliation, drops applied as status='skipped' source='reconciled'
Backend: guards with AIService configured check, skips if not
Backend: job status unchanged on failure (graceful degradation)
Backend: job event logged 'Batch review dropped N items'
CLI: runs when --reconcile flag used
Barrel export updated in services/index.ts
5+ unit tests covering all parse scenarios
Integration test in agent.test.ts
warnings: Pre-mortem R4: Reuse JSON extraction pattern from parseMeetingExtractionResponse (strip fences, find braces).
Pre-mortem R7: Keep test concerns separate — mock LLM only for batchLLMReview tests, mock storage only for memory tests.

userStories.title: Wire prior items from recent meetings in backend
userStories.description: Load recent meeting items via loadRecentMeetingBatch() and pass as priorItems to extraction. File: packages/apps/backend/src/services/agent.ts, in runProcessingSession() (~line 430), before calling runProcessingSessionTestable(). Import loadRecentMeetingBatch from meeting-reconciliation.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: Loads recent meetings via loadRecentMeetingBatch() before processing
Converts to PriorItem[] format (type + text + source per item)
Passes as options.priorItems to processing session
try/catch: failure logs warning, extraction runs without exclusion list
Unit test: priorItems length matches sum of items from recent batch
Existing agent tests pass unchanged
